label: About
title: About Scan Gear Resin Medusa
short_blurb: A daily generated front page where the image is the interface.
body: Daily Frontpage is a daily generated interactive front page. Each edition starts from recent saved signals and source research, becomes a new image-led scene, and stays explorable in the archive. The image is the interface: visible marks, objects, and surfaces in the scene open the real sources that shaped the day.

This iteration began with 21 recent saved-signal files and 63 captured links, then narrowed the page to 9 source windows around @ErenChenAI: A Chinese girl DIY’d a gear-driven 3D-printed Medusa, and it’s oddly mesmerizing to... and A structured light 3D scan of Shenzhen hardware hacker and DIY enthusiast Naomi Wu wearing a..., plus 7 more. A source image from upload.wikimedia.org helped set the palette, contrast, edge behavior, and gesture. The scene became Scan Gear Resin Medusa: A fashion/material macro plate in shallow macro grammar: a dark graphite ground holds one large cropped off-white printed resin surface, bright like a structured-light scan but not a portrait. After the image was made, a plate-reading pass mapped 9 visible regions and the mask audit tightened them against left serpentine toothed hinge coils, large lower-left gear aperture, and broad pale scanned body-slab with triangular facets.

typography.rationale: Bricolage Grotesque and Space Grotesk fit record, arcade, and listening-room editions while Fraunces keeps source captions tactile.
